subject: Add kubernetes-node group for workers
commit_message:
Add kubernetes-node group for workers

Moved all kubernetes nodes from the kubernetes to kubernetes-node group,
and made the kubernetes group a parent of both kubernetes-node and
kubernetes-control-plane.
The reason is that we still need to have separate groups for
control planes and for nodes, but some
operations are performed equally on any kind of kubernetes
cluster member, and currently one would need to separately include all
related groups one by one. Example of
such common operation is installation of docker, that was not
performed for kubernetes-control-plane group, so that
docker was not installed on control planes that were neither infra nor nodes.
The side effect of this change is that if infrastructure
server is also a cluster member, it lands in a kubernetes group by default,
and node specific setup would be performed on it.
For that reason, playbook is modified to perform this setup on all
kubernetes cluster members except the infra server.

Change-Id: Ic827002d28e535334dbab7e5ad4aed8aa95c97a9
Issue-ID: OOM-1778
Signed-off-by: Michal Zegan <m.zegan@samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Actually wondering if it doesn't also make sense to add the kubernetes-etcd group? in case of production clusters you rather don't colocate etcd and nodes, and it is now relatively easy to do.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Actually wondering if it doesn't also make sense to add the kubernetes-etcd group? in case of production clusters you rather don't colocate etcd and nodes, and it is now relatively easy to do.

Or is the kubernetes-control-plane group actually a kubernetes-master group now that would be equal to kubernetes-etcd? So can deal both with just kubernetes-master group?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Actually wondering if it doesn't also make sense to add the kubernetes-etcd group? in case of production clusters you rather don't colocate etcd and nodes, and it is now relatively easy to do.
> 
> Or is the kubernetes-control-plane group actually a kubernetes-master group now that would be equal to kubernetes-etcd? So can deal both with just kubernetes-master group?
Well they are separate roles not without a reason. Especially if we want to have high availability support in the future, we need a way to have etcd cluster separate from both master and nodes (especially nodes).
It may be something not so relevant here, but it seems a natural extension.

> > 
> > Or is the kubernetes-control-plane group actually a kubernetes-master group now that would be equal to kubernetes-etcd? So can deal both with just kubernetes-master group?
> Well they are separate roles not without a reason. Especially if we want to have high availability support in the future, we need a way to have etcd cluster separate from both master and nodes (especially nodes).
> It may be something not so relevant here, but it seems a natural extension.

Yes but for now etcd would be in master. So Could kubernetes-control-plane be named as kubernetes-master? And in addition we could have kubernetes-etcd when we need it.

> > It may be something not so relevant here, but it seems a natural extension.

> Yes but for now etcd would be in master. So Could kubernetes-control-plane be named as kubernetes-master? And in addition we could have kubernetes-etcd when we need it.

Well not sure then. I could as well make kubernetes-etcd role defaulting to infra, and not require further extension of this.

Patch Set 1:

Ok, i will add the kubernetes-etcd as a new group. I believe it makes the most sense, and will not require extensive changes later.
Uploaded patch set 2: Patch Set 1 was rebased.

Patch Set 2: Verified+1
